zh.East Grinstead, Sussex, England: 英國素可易亞斯特 格林斯亞德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isolda Heryng於從1380年到1411年出生在英國多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同時，她的父親Sir William de Heron62歲，母親Elizabeth de Say55歲。她於1403年與John de la Lynde I在英國英格蘭素可易亞斯特 格林斯亞德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1456年2月4日在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isolda Heryng于从1380年到1411年出生在英国多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同时，她的父亲Sir William de Heron62岁，母亲Elizabeth de Say55岁。她于1403年与John de la Lynde I在英国英格兰素可易亞斯特 格林斯亞德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1456年2月4日在她的家乡，埋葬在英国多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同。

nameMeanings.name: Heryng
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hasso, Alfons, Alois, Arno, Ernst, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Guenther, Kurt, Ludwig, Manfred.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German hærinc ‘herring’, German Hering, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, particularly a seller of herrings, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a herring. In some cases the Jewish surname is artificial. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German and Jewish variant Häring. Compare Haring .

German and Dutch: habitational name from any of several places and farmhouses so named, for instance near Zeesse in Overijssel, originally inhabited by a certain Here or Hero and his kin. Here or Hero is a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the element heri ‘army’.

variants.name: Haring
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (mainly Häring); Dutch: from Middle Low German hārinc ‘herring’, German Hering, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, particularly a seller of herringss, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a herring. In some cases the Jewish surname is artificial. Compare Haering and Hering .
Austrian German: nickname for a skinny person (like a herring; see 1 above).
German (Häring): habitational name from Häring (Bavaria, Tyrol) or Häringen (Baden-Württemberg).

variants.name: Herring
meanings: English (London), Dutch, and German: metonymic occupational name for a herring fisher or for a seller of the fish, or perhaps for someone who habitually ate herring, from Middle English hering (Old English hǣring, hēring), Dutch haring, Middle High German hærinc. In some cases it may have been a nickname in the sense of a trifle, something of little value, a meaning which is found in medieval phrases and proverbial expressions such as ‘to like neither herring nor barrel’, i.e. not to like something at all.
German: habitational name from Herringen in Westphalia.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Hering  .

variants.name: Heuring
meanings: German: variant of Höring, probably a vowel variant of Hering .

variants.name: Hoerig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt.

German (Hörig): nickname from Middle High German hœrec ‘obedient, in bondage’, Middle Low German horich ‘obedient, belonging to’. In some cases from Middle Low German horink ‘child born out of wedlock’.

nameMeanings.name: Isolda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The name of the tragic mistress of Tristan in the Arthurian romances. There are several versions of the story. The main features are that the beautiful Isolde, an Irish princess, is betrothed to the aged King Mark of Cornwall. However, through accidentally drinking a magic potion, she and the young Cornish knight Tristan fall in love, with tragic consequences. The story has exercised a powerful hold on the European imagination. The name was relatively common in Britain in the Middle Ages, but is much rarer today.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isolda Heryng was born from 1380 to 1411, in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England, her father, Sir William de Heron, was 62 and her mother, Elizabeth de Say, was 55. She married John de la Lynde I in 1403, in East Grinstead, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 4 February 1456, in her hometown, and was buried in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isolda Heryng est née du 1380 au 1411, à Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Heron, avait 62 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Say, avait 55 ans. Elle a épousé John de la Lynde I en 1403, à East Grinstead, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 février 1456, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, Angleterre.

ja.East Grinstead, Sussex, England: イギリススセックスエアスト グリンステアド
ja.lifeSummary: Isolda Heryngがから1380年まで1411年にイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンで生まれた時，父Sir William de Heronは62歳，母Elizabeth de Sayは55歳であった。1403年にイギリスイギリススセックスエアスト グリンステアドでJohn de la Lynde Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1456年2月4日に故郷で死去し，イギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isolda Heryng(이)가 1380년 부터 1411년 까지 영국 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Heron(은)는 62세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Say(은)는 55세였다. 그녀는 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 이스트 그린스테아드에서 John de la Lynde I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1456년 2월 4일 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John de la Lynde I em 1403, em East Grinstead, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isolda Heryng родилась с 1380 по 1411 в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Heron было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Say, 55. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de la Lynde I в 1403 г. в местности эаст гринстид, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 4 февраля 1456 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия.
